data.title: On Holding (NYSE:ONON): Assessing Valuation Potential After Recent Share Price Rebound
data.description:
On Holding (NYSE:ONON) has seen its stock deliver a mixed performance across recent timeframes. The stock moved higher by 12% in the past month, though it remains down over the past year. Investors are paying close attention to these dynamics as the company navigates a changing retail landscape.

See our latest analysis for On Holding.

While On Holding’s 1-year share price return is in the red, recent momentum is shifting as the stock rebounded nearly 12% over the past month. Long-term holders have still enjoyed a remarkable 124% total shareholder return over three years, which may indicate growth potential as perceptions improve.

If you’re looking for more opportunities beyond athletic brands, now may be a good time to broaden your investing radar and discover fast growing stocks with high insider ownership

With recent gains and long-term outperformance, investors may wonder whether On Holding is currently undervalued or if the share price already reflects the company's growth prospects. Is this a buying opportunity, or is the market anticipating future expansion?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 28.2% Undervalued

At $43.99, On Holding trades well below the narrative’s fair value estimate of $61.29. This frames the stock’s latest rally in a provocative new light for growth-focused investors. These numbers suggest that the consensus narrative sees significant room for upward re-rating if the company executes on its strategies.

The acceleration in DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) and e-commerce channels, with DTC reaching new highs (41.1% of sales in Q2 and up 54% year over year), gives On more control over brand, pricing, and customer data while increasing gross and EBITDA margins. This operational catalyst is likely to further expand profitability as DTC continues its mix shift.

Read the complete narrative.

Want a glimpse at the expectation engine fueling this valuation? The narrative hinges on rapid revenue growth, a surge in future profit margins, and a bold re-rating in profitability multiples. The secret formula behind this price target? The full narrative unpacks the growth assumptions propelling On Holding’s fair value sky-high—see which forecasts have bulls talking.

Result: Fair Value of $61.29 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, sustained reliance on premium pricing or rapid store expansion could backfire if consumer demand shifts or operational challenges arise. This could put margins and growth targets at risk.

Find out about the key risks to this On Holding narrative.

Another View: Market Multiples Raise Caution

Looking at the company’s price tag through the lens of the price-to-earnings ratio paints a more cautious picture. On Holding trades at 52.6x earnings, almost double the peer average of 28.3x and the US Luxury industry’s 20x benchmark. The fair ratio, which the market could eventually move toward, is 26.8x. This wide gap suggests investors may be paying a significant premium. Does this raise the risk that expectations are running ahead of fundamentals?

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

data.title: Will CarMax's (KMX) New Store Expansion Offset Legal Uncertainty in Its Growth Story?
data.description:
• Earlier this month, CarMax, Inc. announced the grand opening of its first Arkansas store in Rogers, marking its 255th location nationwide and highlighting new customer-focused offerings like at-home vehicle pickup and the Offer Watch tool.
    In the days following significant corporate changes, multiple law firms disclosed the filing and expansion of securities class action lawsuits against CarMax, alleging that management made materially false or misleading statements regarding growth prospects and finance portfolio quality during recent quarters.
  • We'll examine how this wave of legal scrutiny, amidst operational changes, influences CarMax's investment narrative and future business outlook.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

CarMax Investment Narrative Recap

To be a CarMax shareholder right now, you need to believe in the company’s ability to grow its omnichannel used car business and improve profitability by expanding digital and in-store integration, even as it faces heightened legal scrutiny. The recent wave of securities class action lawsuits, combined with ongoing leadership changes, introduces a material near-term risk as it could distract management from focusing fully on operational improvements, while the most important short-term catalyst remains the company’s ability to stabilize and grow comparable store sales amid challenging macroeconomic conditions.

One recent announcement with direct relevance is the introduction of enhanced appraisal experience tools such as at-home vehicle pickup and the Offer Watch feature, which aim to improve customer engagement and empower sellers with greater flexibility. These updates are designed to support CarMax’s key growth catalyst of expanding its digital and omnichannel capabilities, helping drive volume and customer satisfaction as the business adapts to evolving consumer needs.

In contrast, investors should be aware that ongoing litigation alleging misleading statements regarding growth prospects could...

Read the full narrative on CarMax (it's free!)

CarMax's narrative projects $29.8 billion revenue and $919.9 million earnings by 2028. This requires 1.3% yearly revenue growth and a $361.4 million earnings increase from $558.5 million today.

Uncover how CarMax's forecasts yield a $39.83 fair value, a 3% upside to its current price.

data.title: How Might Kinnevik's (OM:KINV B) Leadership Transition Test Investor Confidence in Its Long-Term Vision?
data.description:
• Kinnevik AB has announced that CEO Georgi Ganev will step down, remaining in his role for up to twelve months or until a successor is appointed.
  • This transition marks a pivotal moment for Kinnevik, as executive changes often influence both leadership direction and investor perceptions.
  • We’ll explore how the planned CEO departure could shape Kinnevik’s investment narrative amid heightened interest in executive stability.

This technology could replace computers: discover 28 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Kinnevik's Investment Narrative?

To believe in Kinnevik as an investment, you need to buy into its potential for rapid revenue and earnings growth, a trait reflected in recent analyst forecasts and recent profits after a period of losses. The core thesis relies on Kinnevik’s portfolio of high-growth businesses, but recent index removals and a relatively inexperienced board have raised some questions about stability. The announced CEO transition could weigh on investor sentiment in the short term, especially given the pivotal role leadership plays in shaping both strategy and confidence. However, judging by recent muted share price reaction, the leadership change does not appear to materially shift immediate catalysts such as the acceleration in revenue and earnings, or reshape the current risk profile dominated by board turnover and lumpy earnings quality. The main risk remains whether a new CEO and recently refreshed board can execute on ambitious growth targets and restore market confidence. On the other hand, Kinnevik’s board inexperience is a risk worth a closer look.

According our valuation report, there's an indication that Kinnevik's share price might be on the expensive side.

data.title: Victory Square Technologies (CSE:VST) shareholders are still up 426% over 3 years despite pulling back 14% in the past week
data.description:
Victory Square Technologies Inc. (CSE:VST) shareholders might be concerned after seeing the share price drop 14% in the last week. But over the last three years the stock has shone bright like a diamond. The longer term view reveals that the share price is up 426% in that period. So you might argue that the recent reduction in the share price is unremarkable in light of the longer term performance. The share price action could signify that the business itself is dramatically improved, in that time.

Since the long term performance has been good but there's been a recent pullback of 14%, let's check if the fundamentals match the share price.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

While markets are a powerful pricing mechanism, share prices reflect investor sentiment, not just underlying business performance. One way to examine how market sentiment has changed over time is to look at the interaction between a company's share price and its earnings per share (EPS).

Victory Square Technologies became profitable within the last three years. Given the importance of this milestone, it's not overly surprising that the share price has increased strongly.

The company's earnings per share (over time) is depicted in the image below (click to see the exact numbers).

This free interactive report on Victory Square Technologies' earnings, revenue and cash flow is a great place to start, if you want to investigate the stock further.

Advertisement

A Different Perspective

We're pleased to report that Victory Square Technologies shareholders have received a total shareholder return of 390% over one year. That gain is better than the annual TSR over five years, which is 3%. Therefore it seems like sentiment around the company has been positive lately. In the best case scenario, this may hint at some real business momentum, implying that now could be a great time to delve deeper. I find it very interesting to look at share price over the long term as a proxy for business performance. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. Even so, be aware that Victory Square Technologies is showing 3 warning signs in our investment analysis , and 1 of those is significant...

data.title: Weighing DoorDash After New Expansion Moves and a 25% Drop in Share Price
data.description:
• If you have ever wondered whether DoorDash stock is worth its price tag, you are not alone. Understanding what drives its current valuation is key before taking the plunge.
  • The stock has seen some wild swings lately, climbing 4.6% this week but still down 25.4% over the past month. However, it remains up 16.2% so far this year and 259.4% over three years.
  • Recent headlines have spotlighted DoorDash's strategic moves, including expansion into new markets and partnerships with major retailers. These developments have fueled both excitement and debate, helping to explain the company's volatile share price as investors react to the evolving business landscape surrounding food delivery.
  • According to our checks, DoorDash currently scores 3 out of 6 for relative undervaluation. Up next, we will break down the good, the bad, and the better ways to judge whether DoorDash is truly a buy, saving the most powerful approach for last.

DoorDash delivered 9.9% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Hospitality industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: DoorDash Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model estimates a company’s intrinsic value by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s value. This approach helps investors gauge what a business is really worth based on its ability to generate cash over time.

DoorDash’s current Free Cash Flow stands at $2.0 billion. Analysts project that annual cash flows will increase steadily, with Simply Wall St extrapolating growth beyond the next five years. By 2029, Free Cash Flow is expected to reach approximately $6.2 billion, with longer-term forecasts suggesting continued robust growth.

This DCF analysis, using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, calculates DoorDash’s estimated intrinsic value at $308.84 per share. This suggests the stock is currently trading at a 35.8% discount, and the model indicates that DoorDash stock appears to be undervalued relative to its future cash generation potential.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests DoorDash is undervalued by 35.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 920 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for DoorDash.

Approach 2: DoorDash Price vs Earnings

The Price-to-Earnings (PE) ratio is an effective metric for valuing profitable companies like DoorDash, as it measures how much investors are willing to pay today for a dollar of the company's earnings. For companies generating positive net income, the PE ratio often reflects both the market’s growth expectations and the perceived risks in future performance. Generally, higher growth rates and lower risks justify higher PE ratios. More uncertainty or slower growth can lead to lower "normal" or "fair" PE multiples.

Currently, DoorDash trades at a PE ratio of 99.1x, which is significantly higher than the Hospitality industry average of 21.3x and the average for its peers at 34.5x. At first glance, this premium might raise eyebrows. However, headline comparisons can sometimes miss the unique strengths or risks of individual companies.

This is where Simply Wall St’s proprietary Fair Ratio is relevant. The Fair Ratio, calculated at 46.8x for DoorDash, is specifically designed to reflect expectations around growth, profitability, market cap, and risk factors tailored to the company rather than relying only on peer or industry averages. By considering a fuller financial picture, the Fair Ratio offers a more nuanced benchmark for a company’s true value.

Comparing DoorDash’s current PE ratio (99.1x) with its Fair Ratio (46.8x) suggests the stock is trading at a substantial premium relative to what would be considered fair value under these metrics.

Result: OVERVALUED

PE ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1443 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your DoorDash Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let's introduce you to Narratives. A Narrative is simply the story or perspective that you, as an investor, attach to a company's future, connecting what you believe about DoorDash's growth, margins, and risks to your own financial forecasts and Fair Value estimates, rather than relying only on historical data or analyst averages.

Narratives are powerful because they link a company's story, such as expected expansion into new markets, innovation in AI, or evolving customer behavior, directly to dynamic financial models and a Fair Value figure. This transforms abstract forecasts into actionable, personalized investment signals, letting you decide if the current price offers a good opportunity based on your outlook, not just consensus.

On Simply Wall St's Community page, millions of investors are already using Narratives to compare their Fair Value to today's price, helping them decide whether to buy or sell. Narratives update automatically as new information like earnings or news comes in, keeping your analysis fresh and relevant.

For instance, one Narrative for DoorDash assumes ambitious international growth and aggressive profit margin expansion, leading to a Fair Value estimate of $360, while a more cautious Narrative factoring in rising costs and competitive threats values the company at just $205. This proves there is no "one-size-fits-all" answer and highlights how your perspective drives your valuation.

Do you think there's more to the story for DoorDash? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Assessing Acadian Asset Management After 74% Jump and Digital Asset Market Expansion in 2025
data.description:
• Ever found yourself wondering if Acadian Asset Management's impressive run means it is actually undervalued, or if the price has already baked in future growth? You're not alone. That is exactly what we'll unpack in this article.
  • Over the last year, Acadian's stock price has jumped 44%, including a recent swing of -9.0% in the last month, alongside a notable year-to-date gain of 73.9% that has many investors buzzing about growth potential and the possible risks.
  • Recent news surrounding the firm's expansion into new markets and innovative digital asset strategies has continued to drive attention. Market commentators are divided on whether this marks a bold new chapter or adds extra volatility. These headlines are fueling discussion about how much optimism is already reflected in the share price.
  • When we ran Acadian through our valuation checks, it scored a modest 1 out of 6, meaning it's only undervalued by one measure. Next, we'll break down how each traditional valuation approach stacks up. There is an even smarter way to think about stock value that we'll cover at the end.

Acadian Asset Management scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Acadian Asset Management Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns valuation model focuses on how efficiently a company employs shareholder capital, specifically by comparing its return on equity to the cost of equity. If a business generates returns that significantly exceed its cost to raise and use capital, it can create sustained value for its shareholders.

For Acadian Asset Management, the numbers paint a striking picture. The average return on equity stands out at an impressive 1,446.67%, far higher than typical industry levels. The model calculates a stable EPS of $1.29 per share, backed by a stable book value of $0.54 per share (both metrics sourced from the company’s median performance over the past five years). The cost of equity is remarkably low at $0.01 per share, yielding an excess return of $1.28 per share.

Despite these standout return metrics, the Excess Returns model estimates an intrinsic value that is 127.9% lower than the current share price. This means that, according to this approach, Acadian’s current market valuation is running far ahead of the underlying returns generated on invested equity. Investors should take note, as the market appears to be pricing in a lot of optimism beyond what the business fundamentals seem to support.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests Acadian Asset Management may be overvalued by 127.9%. Discover 920 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Acadian Asset Management.

Approach 2: Acadian Asset Management Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like Acadian Asset Management, the Price-to-Earnings (PE) ratio is a commonly used measure of value. This multiple captures how much investors are willing to pay today for a dollar of earnings, making it a useful gauge for comparing companies with consistent profits. Growth expectations and perceived risk play a major role in what investors consider a “normal” or “fair” PE ratio. Higher expected growth or lower risk typically commands a higher PE, while greater risk or limited growth prospects warrant a lower multiple.

Acadian currently trades at a PE ratio of 18.24x. This is below the Capital Markets industry average of 23.87x and also under the peer average of 12.50x. At first glance, this positioning might make the stock look attractively priced relative to broader market norms. However, there is more to the story than headline comparisons.

Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" aims to set a more accurate baseline by factoring in Acadian’s unique growth profile, profit margins, risk characteristics, and market cap, rather than relying solely on broad industry or peer groups. This helps ensure that the valuation reflects core fundamentals and company-specific drivers. When comparing Acadian’s current PE ratio to its Fair Ratio, it becomes clear whether the market is assigning an appropriate value or if there is a potential mispricing worth attention.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

PE ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1443 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Acadian Asset Management Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let's introduce you to Narratives. A Narrative is simply your story about a company, connecting your expectations and beliefs about its future with concrete financial forecasts, such as projected revenue, profit margins, and fair value estimates. Think of Narratives as an easy, interactive tool available on Simply Wall St’s Community page, designed to make sophisticated investing accessible to everyone. Narratives link the company’s real-world story and prospects to a dynamic financial forecast, which then produces a personalized fair value calculation.

With Narratives, investors can easily see when a company's current price offers a compelling buy or signposts caution by comparing the Fair Value from a Narrative to the latest market Price. What sets Narratives apart is their ability to adapt live as new information like news or earnings is released, so your view always stays relevant. For example, on Acadian Asset Management, one investor’s Narrative might point to much higher upside potential based on bullish growth forecasts, while another’s could reveal more risk and a lower fair value, all in one easy-to-use platform.

Do you think there's more to the story for Acadian Asset Management? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: There's No Escaping Starcore International Mines Ltd.'s (TSE:SAM) Muted Revenues Despite A 39% Share Price Rise
data.description:
Starcore International Mines Ltd. (TSE:SAM) shares have continued their recent momentum with a 39% gain in the last month alone. The last 30 days were the cherry on top of the stock's 344% gain in the last year, which is nothing short of spectacular.

In spite of the firm bounce in price, Starcore International Mines' price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 2x might still make it look like a strong buy right now compared to the wider Metals and Mining industry in Canada, where around half of the companies have P/S ratios above 6.3x and even P/S above 45x are quite common. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the highly reduced P/S.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

View our latest analysis for Starcore International Mines

Advertisement

How Has Starcore International Mines Performed Recently?

Revenue has risen at a steady rate over the last year for Starcore International Mines, which is generally not a bad outcome. It might be that many expect the respectable revenue performance to degrade, which has repressed the P/S. If that doesn't eventuate, then existing shareholders may have reason to be optimistic about the future direction of the share price.

Although there are no analyst estimates available for Starcore International Mines, take a look at this free data-rich visualisation to see how the company stacks up on earnings, revenue and cash flow.

What Are Revenue Growth Metrics Telling Us About The Low P/S?

Starcore International Mines' P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's expected to deliver very poor growth or even falling revenue, and importantly, perform much worse than the industry.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a decent 3.1% gain to the company's revenues. The latest three year period has also seen a 22% overall rise in revenue, aided somewhat by its short-term performance. Therefore, it's fair to say the revenue growth recently has been respectable for the company.

This is in contrast to the rest of the industry, which is expected to grow by 50% over the next year, materially higher than the company's recent medium-term annualised growth rates.

With this information, we can see why Starcore International Mines is trading at a P/S lower than the industry. Apparently many shareholders weren't comfortable holding on to something they believe will continue to trail the wider industry.

What We Can Learn From Starcore International Mines' P/S?

Starcore International Mines' recent share price jump still sees fails to bring its P/S alongside the industry median. While the price-to-sales ratio shouldn't be the defining factor in whether you buy a stock or not, it's quite a capable barometer of revenue expectations.

Our examination of Starcore International Mines confirms that the company's revenue trends over the past three-year years are a key factor in its low price-to-sales ratio, as we suspected, given they fall short of current industry expectations. At this stage investors feel the potential for an improvement in revenue isn't great enough to justify a higher P/S ratio. Unless the recent medium-term conditions improve, they will continue to form a barrier for the share price around these levels.

There are also other vital risk factors to consider before investing and we've discovered 5 warning signs for Starcore International Mines that you should be aware of.

If companies with solid past earnings growth is up your alley, you may wish to see this free collection of other companies with strong earnings growth and low P/E ratios.

After surging to an all-time high, RBC Bearings has kept up strong momentum, with the share price up nearly 49% year-to-date and a standout three-year total shareholder return of almost 94%. Investors are clearly taking notice of the company’s robust execution and expansion in Aerospace and Defense, reinforcing positive sentiment following its recent quarterly outperformance.

Given the sector’s renewed energy, you might want to see which other companies in Aerospace and Defense are catching investor attention. See the full list for free.

With shares at record highs and analyst optimism growing, the key question now is whether RBC Bearings remains undervalued after its impressive run, or if the market has already priced in the company’s future growth potential.

With a narrative fair value of $472 compared to a last close of $444.97, the favored outlook signals further upside may be in store for RBC Bearings if consensus forecasts play out. Here is the key catalyst driving this view.

“Robust multi-year increases in defense spending, driven by mounting global geopolitical tensions and fleet modernizations, are fueling unprecedented demand for RBC's aerospace components. This is underpinning a record $1B+ backlog and positioning the company for durable top-line growth and long-term contract visibility that should drive sustained revenue and orderbook expansion.”

Looking from a different angle, RBC is trading at a price-to-earnings ratio of 54.3x. This is substantially higher than both the US Machinery industry average, which is 24.8x, and its peers at 27.9x. Even when compared to the fair ratio of 29.6x, it appears expensive, which could signal heightened valuation risk for new investors. Could momentum carry it further, or are expectations too high?

data.title: What Belc (TSE:9974)'s Shareholder Benefits Expansion and Belc Pay Launch Could Mean for Investors
data.description:
• Belc Co., Ltd. recently announced the expansion of its shareholder benefit program, effective February 2026, which introduces the option for shareholders to make donations and revealed plans to launch the Belc Pay electronic money card in February 2027 for use in its stores.
  • This move highlights Belc's emphasis on fostering long-term shareholder engagement and strengthening its commitment to social responsibility and value creation.
  • We’ll explore how the introduction of Belc Pay may reinforce the company’s investment narrative and support shareholder engagement initiatives.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 30 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Belc's Investment Narrative?

To feel confident as a Belc shareholder, it helps to believe in the company's efforts toward sustainable, shareholder-focused growth and a willingness to adapt in a changing consumer environment. The recently announced expansion of the shareholder benefit program, with the addition of donation options and the coming launch of Belc Pay, fits squarely into this story of engagement and modernization. While these initiatives highlight Belc’s interest in deepening customer and investor relationships, the impact on immediate financial catalysts like earnings or revenue growth may be limited until Belc Pay gains traction in 2027. For now, upcoming catalysts remain tied to quarter-by-quarter performance and continued dividend payments, and the biggest risks continue to center around competitive pressure and maintaining profit margins, neither have materially changed yet in light of the new announcement, though the longer-term business profile could shift as these programs develop. Yet, it’s worth remembering that heightened competition in Japanese consumer retail could still weigh on returns.

Belc's share price has been on the slide but might be dropping deeper into value territory. Find out whether it's a bargain at this price.

While some market participants see opportunity in shareholder engagement moves, increased competition and margin pressures should remain on your radar.

data.title: Should Rising Costs Amid Strong Sales Growth Prompt a Closer Look at BBB Foods’ (TBBB) Strategy?
data.description:
• On November 19, 2025, BBB Foods Inc. announced third quarter results, reporting MX$20.28 billion in revenue and a MX$1.42 billion net loss, with revenue continuing to grow year-over-year but profitability impacted by higher share-based compensation costs.
  • Despite strong same-store sales growth of 17.9%, the sharp swing from net income to net loss highlights how increased compensation expenses are influencing the company’s bottom line during this expansion phase.
  • As BBB Foods balances robust sales momentum with rising costs, we'll consider how these earnings results affect the company's investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 30 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

BBB Foods Investment Narrative Recap

Shareholders in BBB Foods must believe in the company's ability to convert rapid revenue growth from aggressive store expansion into sustainable profits, despite significant short-term margin pressures. The latest earnings release does not materially change the biggest catalyst, maturing new stores and same-store sales momentum, or the principal risk, which remains the potential for structural margin dilution if costs outpace expected sales gains.

Of the various recent corporate announcements, the follow-on equity offering completed in February 2024 stands out, as it provided additional capital for expansion and ongoing operational requirements. This move is especially relevant as rising expansion and compensation costs continue to pressure margins even as topline growth accelerates.

However, beneath the impressive sales gains, investors should keep an eye on margin pressures from cost expansions, since ...
